The piece further recommends specific areas for enhanced cooperation, including critical technologies like semiconductors, biotechnology, and space, as well as strengthening maritime security and governance.
mainIdeas: The Quad aims to achieve shared goals in the Indo-Pacific across several key sectors, including infrastructure, energy, and cybersecurity.
The group has established various working groups and initiatives to pursue its multi-sectoral focus.

Key recommendations for the Quad include expanding cooperation in critical technology areas like semiconductors, biotechnology, and space, and strengthening maritime security and governance in the region.

articles.title: Assessing the Latest Developments in the Chinese Economy
articles.metadataRaw: Aug 30, 2023 Anushka Saxena, Amit Kumar, Manoj Kewalramani
articles.summary: China's expected strong economic rebound after ending its Zero-COVID policy has been much weaker than anticipated, revealing systemic issues in the first half of 2023. The economy faces persistent challenges, including soft domestic consumer demand, a fragile property sector, rising local government debt, and declining foreign trade and investment. The government has opted for minor policy adjustments instead of a large stimulus package, while increased state intervention continues to hinder private sector growth.
mainIdeas: China's post-Zero-COVID economic recovery is weaker than expected, with systemic weaknesses appearing in consumer demand, property, and local government finances.
Key factors driving the slowdown include a decline in domestic consumption, a fragile real estate market with links to local government debt, and reduced foreign investment.
The government's limited stimulus measures and ongoing state intervention are impeding a stronger recovery, particularly for the private sector.

articles.title: IPPR Journal Article - Mitigation of Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) in G20
articles.summary: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is a significant global health threat, caused by factors such as irresponsible antibiotic use in humans and animals, and climate change. Despite its severity, responses have largely been limited to health interventions, failing to integrate into broader public policy. The article highlights that effective management of AMR requires global collaborative efforts, noting the current landscape of policies and funding within G20 countries. It emphasizes the urgent need for concerted international cooperation to address this critical issue.
mainIdeas: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is a global health security threat driven by factors beyond just healthcare, including environmental factors.
Current policy responses to AMR are insufficient, primarily focusing on health interventions and lacking broader integration into public policy frameworks.
Addressing AMR effectively requires urgent, concerted action and international cooperation, particularly among G20 nations, to develop comprehensive strategies and funding.

articles.title: IPPR Journal Article - Defining Strategic and Critical Vulnerabilities in Asymmetrical Trade Interdependence
articles.summary: Global economic integration has created situations where some countries hold disproportionate economic power, making economic pressure a common tool in international relations. While nations aim to reduce their economic weaknesses, there's a current lack of clear definitions to differentiate between general trade dependence and truly critical vulnerabilities. This article introduces a practical framework, using a series of tests, to determine when trade in a specific product poses a critical risk to a country. This aims to prevent a one-size-fits-all approach to managing such dependencies.
mainIdeas: Unequal global economic ties empower some countries to use economic pressure against others.
Current policy lacks a clear way to distinguish general trade reliance from critical national vulnerabilities.
The paper introduces a practical framework to identify trade relationships that pose critical risks, aiming for more targeted policy solutions.

articles.title: Mitigation of Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) in G20
articles.summary: Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) poses a recognized global threat to health security, yet current policy responses have been largely ineffective and limited to health-specific interventions. This resistance is fueled by factors such as sustained, irresponsible antibiotic use in both humans and animals, as well as climate change. Tackling AMR effectively demands widespread global collaborative efforts. This analysis reviews AMR's significance, existing policies, national action plans, and funding within G20 countries, underscoring the critical need for concerted international cooperation.
mainIdeas: AMR is a critical global health threat driven by widespread antibiotic misuse and environmental factors like climate change.
Current global and national policies, particularly within the G20, are insufficient and too narrowly focused to effectively combat AMR.
Global, multi-sector cooperation is essential to develop comprehensive and effective strategies for mitigating antimicrobial resistance.

articles.title: Structuring Integrated Theatre Commands for India
articles.summary: India is undertaking a significant military reform to establish Integrated Theatre Commands (ITCs), building on a recently passed bill that streamlines command and control for joint forces. This article details a proposed structure for these ITCs, dividing India's defense into four geographically and threat-based theatres: a Northern Command (China-focused), a Western Command (Pakistan-focused), and two maritime commands for the South-West and South-East. The plan also redefines the military's top-level command structure, transferring operational authority from individual service chiefs to the new theatre commanders to foster better joint planning and execution.
mainIdeas: The Inter-Services Organisations Bill, 2023, is a foundational step for India to implement Integrated Theatre Commands, centralizing command and disciplinary powers over personnel from different services.
India's defense should be reorganized into four distinct theatre commands—North, West, South-West Maritime, and South-East Maritime—each tailored to specific geographic threats and operational needs.

A new top military command system is proposed, including a Joint Commanders and Staff Committee, which shifts operational responsibility from individual service chiefs to the new theatre commanders for enhanced jointness and efficiency.

articles.title: Takshashila Working Paper - IPPR Journal Article - Mitigation of Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) in G20
articles.summary: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) poses a significant global health security threat, yet policy responses have largely been confined to health-related interventions. The article highlights that AMR is driven by global factors like widespread antibiotic misuse and climate change, necessitating comprehensive international collaboration for effective management. It provides an overview of existing AMR policies, national action plans, and funding in G20 countries, emphasizing the urgent need for concerted global action.
mainIdeas: Antimicrobial resistance is a serious global health risk that current policies are not adequately addressing.
Effective management of AMR requires going beyond health-focused interventions to tackle its global drivers, such as antibiotic overuse and climate change.
International cooperation and concerted action, particularly among G20 nations, are crucial for mitigating the threat of AMR.

articles.title: Think20 Policy Brief - Towards a Global Yet Distributed Network of Stockpiles of Essential Medical Supplies
articles.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the urgent need for a coordinated global approach to future health emergencies, especially for less wealthy countries. This policy brief proposes that G20 nations endorse and contribute to a global network of essential medical supply stockpiles. The World Health Organization (WHO) would identify necessary items, determine host countries, and manage the release of supplies through regional hubs during crises. This system aims to ensure a rapid and effective international response.
mainIdeas: The COVID-19 pandemic exposed a critical gap in global preparedness for health emergencies, particularly impacting low-income countries.
G20 countries should establish and contribute to a globally distributed stockpile of essential medical supplies to improve future pandemic response capabilities.
The World Health Organization (WHO) would play a central role in identifying necessary items, determining storage locations, and coordinating the distribution of supplies during a crisis.

articles.title: Takshashila Issue Brief - A Lost Opportunity to Reform the Censor Board
articles.summary: India's Parliament is currently considering minor amendments to film censorship laws, but these changes do not address the core problem of government control over film content. The article argues that the existing state-run Censor Board frequently imposes moral and political views, limiting creative freedom and viewer choice. It proposes a new system that replaces state censorship with a market-based model where private companies, overseen by an independent Indian Movie Authority, certify films based only on age appropriateness. This reform aims to reduce government interference, expand choices for filmmakers and audiences, and effectively manage the growing volume of online content.
mainIdeas: Current amendments to India's film censorship laws are inadequate and fail to address the fundamental problems of state control and potential misuse of power.
A complete overhaul of the film certification system is needed, moving away from government censorship towards a private, market-based model.

This new framework should involve an independent regulatory authority overseeing multiple private certifiers, who would assign ratings based solely on age, thereby promoting artistic freedom and consumer choice.

articles.title: Role of Local Bodies in Strengthening Grassroots Democracy during the Delimitation Exercise
articles.summary: The article analyzes the challenges posed by India's upcoming delimitation exercise, which will redraw electoral constituency boundaries after decades. It highlights concerns about unequal political representation between states, fiscal imbalances where economically stronger states contribute more but fear losing influence, and issues of governance due to varied population sizes in constituencies. The paper argues that significantly empowering local governments financially and administratively is a crucial solution to address these systemic challenges, promote equitable development, and strengthen democracy at the grassroots level.
mainIdeas: The upcoming redrawing of electoral boundaries in India will bring to light existing problems related to fair representation and the financial relationship between states.
States that have managed population growth effectively and contribute more economically fear a reduction in their political influence, while more populous states are expected to gain more seats.

Strengthening local governments with greater financial resources and administrative control is essential to balance these disparities, improve public service delivery, and make governance more responsive to local needs.

articles.title: Defining Dependence-induced Vulnerabilities in Asymmetrical Trade Interdependence: A Conceptual Framework
articles.summary: Global economic integration has led to an imbalance of economic power, causing some nations to increasingly use economic pressure. This has intensified debates about vulnerabilities stemming from economic dependence, and how to reduce them. However, current decision-making struggles due to a lack of clear definitions for dependence and various types of vulnerabilities, leading to a broad, often ineffective approach. The article proposes a new framework to precisely define and classify critical vulnerabilities in trade between countries.
mainIdeas: Unequal economic power among nations allows some to exert influence through economic means.
Current policy-making on trade vulnerabilities is hampered by a lack of clear definitions for different levels of dependence and risk.
A new framework is needed to accurately identify and address critical vulnerabilities in international trade.

articles.title: SThink20 Policy Brief - Global Teams for Early Warning, Detection, and Response to Disease Outbreaks
articles.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic and other recent outbreaks like SARS and Ebola demonstrated that existing global health regulations have failed to effectively detect and contain disease spread. This policy brief recommends the formation of G20-led global teams specifically designed for early warning, detection, and rapid response to future health threats. These teams would be based in member nations, focusing on data collection and identifying emerging risks, with the G20 also ensuring quick financial assistance to affected countries.
mainIdeas: Current international health regulations are insufficient for effectively managing global disease outbreaks.
The G20 should create specialized global teams to enhance early detection and rapid response to health threats.
Fast access to financial resources is critical for countries impacted by disease outbreaks.

articles.title: Think20 Policy Brief - Sustaining Outer Space: An SSA Data-Sharing Arrangement for the G20
articles.summary: This article emphasizes that Earth’s orbits should be treated as an extension of our planet's environment, making orbital sustainability a key concern for the G20. It points out that the rapidly increasing number of satellites, combined with insufficient monitoring, makes space operations increasingly unsafe. The brief proposes that the G20 establish a Space Situational Awareness (SSA) data-sharing arrangement, overseen by the United Nations, to provide a complete view of activities in space. This collaborative network, utilizing G20 capabilities and commercial technology, aims to significantly improve safety for all space operations.
issues: Space debris and operational safety for satellites
Lack of a coordinated global system for monitoring space activities
mainIdeas: Outer space should be treated as an extension of Earth's environment, making its sustainability a part of the global development agenda.
The growing number of objects in orbit necessitates a unified, global approach to track space activities and prevent collisions.
The G20 should lead an initiative to create a shared data system for Space Situational Awareness (SSA) to improve safety and management in space.

articles.title: Sustaining Outer Space: An SSA Data-Sharing Arrangement for the G20
articles.summary: The article emphasizes that Earth's orbits are an extension of our planet's environment, making orbital sustainability a crucial concern for the G20. With the number of satellites set to rise dramatically, there's an urgent need for better mechanisms to monitor space activities and ensure safety. The author proposes that the G20 integrate outer space into its Sustainable Development Goals agenda and establish a Space Situational Awareness (SSA) data-sharing arrangement. This arrangement, leveraging G20 member capabilities and commercial technologies, would create a comprehensive network to enhance safety for spacecraft and astronauts alike.
mainIdeas: The G20 must recognize outer space sustainability as an environmental imperative and incorporate it into the Sustainable Development Goals.
The rapid increase in orbiting satellites demands a robust, collaborative system to monitor space activities and ensure operational safety.
A G20-led Space Situational Awareness (SSA) data-sharing arrangement, utilizing member states' resources and commercial technology, is essential for creating a safer orbital environment.

articles.title: India’s Biotechnology Ecosystem: A SWOT Analysis
articles.summary: India considers biotechnology vital for its national progress and security. A review of the country's biotech industry highlights its advantages, such as government support, a large workforce, and cost-effective expansion capabilities. However, it suffers from low investment, limited basic research, an underdeveloped regulatory system, and a shortage of specialized talent. To strengthen this sector, the study recommends increasing funds, improving workforce skills, and enhancing global partnerships.
issues: Challenges in Scientific Research and Investment
mainIdeas: India's biotechnology sector has inherent strengths but is hindered by underinvestment and a lack of skilled professionals.
Key barriers include insufficient funding, a weak regulatory framework, and a need for more fundamental research.
To grow the sector, India must boost funding, enhance workforce skills, and expand international collaborations.

articles.title: Takshashila Policy Advisory - Proposals for the U.N’s New Agenda for Peace
articles.metadataRaw: Jun 8, 2023 Shambhavi Naik, Pranav R Satyanath, Shrikrishna Upadhyaya, Saurabh Todi
articles.summary: This article outlines three proposals for updating global security frameworks to address modern threats. It suggests a Global No-First Use Treaty for nuclear weapons to slow proliferation and arms races, arguing it's more realistic than immediate disarmament. The article also calls for strengthening the Biological Weapons Convention with an expert group and mandatory reporting to counter emerging bioweapon risks from new technologies. Lastly, it proposes a Global Space Situational Awareness Treaty to increase transparency and ensure the safe, sustainable use of outer space amidst growing commercial and military activities.
mainIdeas: Existing international treaties are no longer sufficient to address current security challenges, particularly those stemming from new technologies and changing global dynamics.
Focusing on realistic and actionable measures, such as a pledge against the first use of nuclear weapons and mandatory reporting for biological threats, is crucial for improving global stability.
Increasing transparency and fostering global data sharing are vital for managing risks, preventing weaponization, and ensuring the peaceful and sustainable use of critical domains like outer space.

articles.title: Analysing India’s Position on the Information Technology Agreement
articles.metadataRaw: Jun 7, 2023 Satya Shoova Sahu, Miheer Karandikar
articles.summary: India has been a signatory to the WTO's Information Technology Agreement (ITA-1) since 1997, committing to eliminate tariffs on IT products, but has recently faced a WTO ruling for imposing tariffs that go against these commitments. India argues that ITA-1 negatively impacted its domestic IT manufacturing and increased its reliance on imports. However, the analysis suggests India's manufacturing issues are more due to a lack of complementary industrial and investment policies rather than solely the ITA-1 itself. The article recommends India uphold its ITA-1 commitments, explore joining the expanded ITA-2, and use free trade agreements to integrate into global IT supply chains.
mainIdeas: India's current tariff policies on IT products are inconsistent with its international trade commitments under the ITA-1.
The problems in India's domestic IT manufacturing sector are primarily caused by insufficient industrial and investment policies, not just the ITA-1.
India should honor its ITA-1 obligations and use new policy tools like investment incentives and free trade agreements to boost its IT manufacturing and integrate into global supply chains.

articles.title: Think20 Policy Brief - BWC Scientific Experts Group to Combat Biological Threats
articles.summary: Rapid advancements in biotechnology have made it easier to manipulate microorganisms, creating new biosecurity risks that current systems are not equipped to assess. The COVID-19 pandemic demonstrated the severe potential for harm from biological threats, similar to what a bioweapon could unleash. This policy brief proposes establishing a Scientific Expert Group within the Biological Weapons Convention. This group would evaluate risks from new technologies, monitor emerging biological threats, and suggest necessary countermeasures.
mainIdeas: Current systems are insufficient to assess biosecurity risks posed by rapidly advancing biotechnology, as highlighted by the COVID-19 pandemic.
A dedicated Scientific Expert Group is needed within the Biological Weapons Convention to proactively assess new technological risks and monitor evolving biological threats.
This group would recommend practical countermeasures to protect against both natural and man-made biological dangers.

articles.title: Takshashila Issue Brief - The Quad Agenda for Semiconductors, Biotechnology, and Space
articles.summary: The article discusses the Quad's expanding focus beyond traditional security to critical economic and technological areas following a recent leaders' summit. It argues that an economic agenda, built on "bubbles of trust," is essential for strengthening shared security and geopolitical interests in the Indo-Pacific. The piece outlines specific recommendations for collaboration in semiconductors, biotechnology, and space, aiming to create a more resilient and open global economic system.
mainIdeas: The Quad should prioritize economic cooperation in critical technologies to strengthen shared security and geopolitical goals in the Indo-Pacific region.
Member countries should form "bubbles of trust" to facilitate collaboration in semiconductor manufacturing, biotechnology, and space, moving beyond national self-sufficiency.
Concrete proposals include diversifying semiconductor supply chains, establishing a biotech manufacturing hub in India, and exploring a joint space station to enhance resilience and innovation.

articles.title: The China-Taiwan Saga
articles.summary: The article examines the strained relationship between China and Taiwan, highlighting China's attempts to isolate Taiwan and the US's supportive actions which heighten tensions. It notes that Taiwanese public opinion firmly opposes unification, and businesses are relocating operations away from mainland China. While China has not abandoned the option of using force, an immediate invasion is not expected, as conflict would severely damage China's economic aspirations. The paper advises countries like India to advocate for peace and stability in the region, and to enhance economic and maritime partnerships with Taiwan without adopting an anti-China stance.
mainIdeas: The relationship between China and Taiwan is increasingly unstable, driven by Chinese pressure and US support for Taiwan.
Despite threats, China is unlikely to invade Taiwan soon because a conflict would severely undermine its economic development goals.
Taiwanese public opinion is strongly against unification, and businesses are shifting away from economic reliance on mainland China.
India should publicly advocate for peace in the Taiwan Strait and build economic and maritime partnerships with Taiwan and other regional countries.

articles.title: Takshashila Discussion Document – An Comparative Study of State Rules Based on the Biological Diversity Act, 2002
articles.metadataRaw: May 16, 2023 Shambhavi Naik, Adithi Gurkar, Rahul Rajasekar, Ruturaj Gowaikar, Atul Kumar Gupta
articles.summary: India's Biological Diversity Act of 2002 was enacted to protect biodiversity, ensure sustainable resource use, and fairly share benefits. However, the Act has been controversial and needs improvements to meet its objectives while allowing for resource utilization. This paper compares the various rules states have implemented to support this national law. The goal is to find best practices and suggest ways to make these regulations consistent across India.
mainIdeas: The national law for biodiversity protection in India needs to be strengthened due to past controversies and implementation issues.
States have created different rules to apply this national law, leading to inconsistencies.
A study of these state rules can help find better ways to implement the law and create a more uniform system nationwide.

articles.title: Time to Launch the US-India Trade Relationship into Another Orbit
articles.metadataRaw: May 15, 2023 Narayan Ramachandran, Pranay Kotasthane, Anupam Manur, Sarthak Pradhan
articles.summary: The US is now India's largest trading partner, presenting a major opportunity to grow trade as both nations focus on building resilient supply chains and reducing dependence on China. With Prime Minister Modi's upcoming visit, the goal is to significantly boost economic ties, targeting $500 billion in bilateral trade by 2030 and $1 trillion by 2040. The article proposes strategies such as expanding ministerial dialogues to include trade officials, fostering state-level partnerships, and focusing on high-technology sectors to achieve these ambitious targets.
mainIdeas: The US and India plan to significantly increase bilateral trade, aiming for $1 trillion by 2040, by building on their strong existing economic relationship.

Achieving these trade goals requires concrete steps like expanding high-level government dialogues to include commerce representatives and encouraging partnerships between individual states in both countries.
A strategic focus on high-technology sectors, resolving business frictions, and promoting people-to-people exchanges are crucial for deepening economic ties and fostering resilient supply chains.

articles.title: Book Chapter - Interrogating Power and Legitimacy in the Information Age from an Indian Perspective
articles.summary: The article examines how the Information Age is fundamentally transforming concepts of power and legitimacy in international relations, noting that many global political structures are still rooted in the Industrial Age. It argues that power is increasingly exercised by influencing human minds and perceptions rather than solely through military or economic might. This shift makes power more accessible, potentially reduces the importance of traditional force, and empowers non-state actors. Furthermore, the article highlights that gaining and maintaining legitimacy in this new age is challenging for states, as digital tools enable easy exposure of failures and the rapid delegitimization of established narratives.
mainIdeas: The Information Age redefines power by shifting its focus from traditional military and economic strength to influencing human cognition and perceptions.
This new form of power is easier to acquire, can be accumulated more quickly, and enables non-state actors, such as internet companies, to exert significant influence in international affairs.
States face growing challenges in maintaining legitimacy because widespread information allows for easy exposure of their shortcomings and the rapid undermining of their narratives by various actors.

articles.title: Think20 Policy Brief - Reducing Data Asymmetry to Strengthen Supply Chains
articles.summary: The article proposes that G20 nations must work together to close information gaps among businesses involved in supply chains to prevent disruptions. It argues that the G20 is ideally placed to create a framework for this, building on its previous experience with data initiatives. The proposal is for the G20's next data initiative to specifically focus on making supply chains more resilient by promoting open and efficient data sharing.
mainIdeas: Uneven access to critical data among supply chain partners is a key reason for disruptions.
The G20 is the most suitable group to address this problem due to its economic influence and past data-sharing efforts.
Future G20 initiatives should prioritize secure and transparent data sharing to improve supply chain resilience globally.

articles.title: The Quad should Commit to a Bio-hub in India
articles.metadataRaw: May 8, 2023 Saurabh Todi, Shambhavi Naik, Dirk Van Der Kley, Daniel Pavlich
articles.summary: Modern biotechnology is poised to revolutionize global industries, offering biological alternatives for a significant portion of current economic inputs, from plastics to food. This field holds immense economic promise, with the power to transform sectors worth trillions, and also carries significant strategic and social importance for national security, health, and energy. To capitalize on these opportunities and reduce potential vulnerabilities, the article suggests that the Quad countries establish a shared biomanufacturing hub in India. This hub would leverage each member's unique capabilities to foster innovation and strengthen crucial supply chains.
issues: Ensuring resilient supply chains for critical emerging technologies
Capitalizing on the economic and strategic potential of biotechnology
mainIdeas: Biotechnology is a rapidly advancing field with the potential to transform major global industries and create vast economic value.
Control over biotechnology's production and intellectual property will be key for future economic growth, national security, and societal well-being.
The Quad should establish a biomanufacturing hub in India to foster innovation, strengthen supply chain resilience, and secure leadership in this vital sector.

articles.title: Think20 Policy Brief - The Case for a G20-Led Climate Change Relocation Insurance System
articles.summary: Climate change is projected to cause widespread population displacement and refugee crises, creating significant humanitarian, economic, and security concerns globally. This policy brief proposes that the G20 establish a Global Climate Change Relocation Insurance (GCCRI) system. This system would allow individuals at risk to purchase insurance to protect their land, property, and livelihoods from climate disasters. The G20 would benefit by addressing historical climate contributions, preventing uncontrolled migration crises, and enabling more organized refugee relocation.
mainIdeas: Climate change will trigger large-scale population movement, posing major humanitarian, economic, and security challenges worldwide.
A Global Climate Change Relocation Insurance (GCCRI) system, led by the G20, is proposed to help individuals buy protection against climate-related losses.
This system aims to prevent chaotic refugee crises, facilitate organized relocation, and address the historical climate responsibilities of developed nations.

articles.title: Takshashila Policy Advisory - Comments to the Parliamentary Standing Committee on Science and Technology, Environment, Forests and Climate Change on Demand for Grants in theUnion Budget for FY 2023-24
articles.metadataRaw: Apr 19, 2023 Shambhavi Naik, Pradeep Mohandas, Sarthak Pradhan, Aditya Ramanathan, Harshit Kukreja, Saurabh Todi
articles.summary: This document offers detailed comments to India's Parliamentary Standing Committee on Science and Technology, Environment, Forests, and Climate Change. It specifically analyzes the budget requests, known as 'Demand for Grants,' for the 2023-24 fiscal year. The analysis covers general budgetary trends across related government ministries and departments, with a particular focus on the Departments of Space, Biotechnology, Science & Technology, Scientific and Industrial Research, and Atomic Energy.
mainIdeas: The document provides expert commentary on proposed government spending for critical science, technology, and environment sectors.
It presents an analysis of financial trends within these key departments to inform parliamentary decision-making.
Specific feedback is given on funding for areas like space research, biotechnology, and atomic energy.

articles.title: Takshashila Discussion Document - Assessing Arguments Against Closer India-US Ties
articles.summary: The article analyzes arguments against closer ties between India and the United States, noting that some Indian factions view the US as an unreliable partner due to historical and ideological reasons. It argues that while some concerns are understandable, others are unfounded and could harm India's crucial security and economic development. The author asserts that India, as a rising power focused on economic growth, critically needs its partnership with the US and Europe for technology, investment, and export markets, highlighting a strategic opportunity for deeper engagement.
mainIdeas: Arguments against closer India-US ties, while sometimes based on historical suspicion, often underestimate India's current strength and the practical benefits of the partnership.
Partnerships with the US and Europe are vital for India's economic development, providing essential technology, investment, and market access.
India is a rising global power with its own agency, and current geopolitical conditions offer a strategic opportunity to strengthen its crucial ties with the US.

articles.title: Strategic Analysis Journal Article - India’s Space Policy and Counter-Space Capabilities
articles.summary: India is an expanding player in the global space industry, driven by national security needs like data collection, satellite communication, and the goal of deterring potential adversaries such as China and Pakistan. This article examines India's existing space infrastructure, including ISRO and private sector involvement, along with its current space assets and anti-satellite capabilities. Based on this, it proposes future directions for India’s space policy and its ability to counter threats in space.
mainIdeas: India's space sector is growing significantly and is a key contributor to the global space economy.
India's space policy and capabilities are primarily shaped by its national security interests, including gathering intelligence, improving communication, and deterring regional adversaries.
The article will analyze India's current space infrastructure and assets to recommend future strategies for its space policy and its ability to respond to threats in space.

articles.title: Takshashila Issue Brief - AI Race Must Not Overlook AI Hardware
articles.summary: While the popular discourse on Artificial Intelligence largely centers on software applications, the article emphasizes the critical importance of AI hardware—the underlying computing infrastructure. Specialized AI chips, designed for functions like training and inference, represent a rapidly growing and differentiating factor in the semiconductor market. The brief proposes that India can strategically enter the AI hardware sector by focusing on manufacturing less complex inference chips. To achieve this, it recommends policy actions such as building dedicated factories, supporting open-source designs, and fostering international collaborations.
mainIdeas: AI hardware, not just software, is a crucial and often overlooked component essential for the continued advancement and performance of AI technology.
Specialized AI chips, particularly for inference, present a significant and growing market opportunity within the global semiconductor industry.
India has a strategic chance to establish itself in the AI hardware sector by prioritizing the production of AI inference chips through focused government initiatives and international partnerships.

articles.title: India-China Border: Tactics, Talks and Transgressions
articles.summary: Chinese military actions along the Line of Actual Control since 2020 have severely damaged mutual trust with India, transforming their bilateral relationship. These actions are part of China's larger goal to assert dominance in Asia, aiming to pressure India into accepting its regional leadership. As tensions are expected to continue, India needs a comprehensive strategy beyond just border control. This strategy should include sustained military readiness, employing unconventional countermeasures, strengthening its naval power, forming alliances against China, and significantly increasing defense spending for modernization.
mainIdeas: China's border aggression is a deliberate strategic move to establish regional dominance, not merely a territorial dispute.
The sustained military standoff has fundamentally broken down trust between India and China, reshaping their future relations.
India must adopt a broad, long-term strategy that includes military modernization, asymmetric warfare, naval development, and strategic partnerships to counter China's regional ambitions.

articles.title: Takshashila Discussion Document-Strengthening research, promoting innovation through richer collaboration
articles.summary: India's G20 Presidency has made strengthening research collaboration a central focus for cooperation. This document was prepared in September 2022 as a submission to the Indian government, suggesting ways to accelerate this collaboration.
mainIdeas: Boosting research collaboration is a core objective of India's G20 Presidency.
A proposal was submitted to the Indian government detailing steps to speed up this international cooperation.
